violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Bagged bulk goods on floor near back door (i.e. flour).

General equipment - Design and construction. — --Food contact surface of equipment did not meet a design characteristic specified in the food service code. -Single use cup left in sugar as a scoop. -Single use aluminum container left in rice flour as scoop. *Scoops should be a washable utensil, and, if left in dry food between uses, stored with handle up and out of product.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. -Cases of single use items stored on floor near back door and on floor below syrup table at front service area.--Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. -Single use soup cups stored on cloth at soup station. -Single use cups stored directly on table top at syrup dispensers.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. -Drink dumped in hand sink. -Drink cup and dough cutter in sink basin upon arrival. *Paper towels available but stored loose on sink edge instead of in paper towel dispenser.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -Floor/wall trim is not prepsent through front service area or in mop closet.
Clean dry location, protected (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pt deducted)
